methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros, a seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Daniel Kultran and Grant Verstandig, has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA), with five leading privates including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to estimate the next funding stage, yielding $75M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median of this set is $150M, with a range of $110M–$200M.

These methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $64M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $112M. The dispersion between methods reflects uncertainty in both the applicable stage-progression multiple and peer selection within an active but still-maturing frontier technology sector. Key sensitivities include the timing and terms of the next funding round, which will anchor realized valuation, and whether Claros's technology maturity and market positioning align with the Series A cohort or warrant a more conservative stage-based approach.
